title: Simira salvadorensis

intro: Simira salvadorensis, also known as Guanacaste tree, is a deciduous tree species native to Central America, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and long, straight trunk. The health benefits of Simira salvadorensis include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Simira salvadorensis include flavonoids, tannins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Simira salvadorensis is often used to make tea, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
